description:
Surfaced during the mutagen-835 SpecLedEx-adoption orchestration (verifier on mutagen-835.2.7 and the mutagen-835.3 auditor both observed it).

Symptom: `make smoke` / `mix test` (which run `--max-failures 1` with a RANDOM seed) intermittently fails in the in-process mutation pipeline / coverage suite — flagged at coverage_runner_test.exs:318. It is timing/telemetry-sensitive and order-dependent (an earlier sibling test leaks a tmp dir / cover state). It is NOT a regression — it reproduces independent of any spec change and passes deterministically under `mix test --seed 0`.

Impact: any orchestration/CI step that treats a single random-seed `make smoke` failure as a hard gate will flake. Throughout mutagen-835 the workaround was to re-run or use `--seed 0`.

Likely fix direction: make the coverage/cover-server teardown deterministic (ensure `:cover.stop/0` + tmp-dir cleanup is idempotent and not leaked across tests), or tag/isolate the offending test so async ordering can't interleave it. See also the auto-memory note `flaky-test-random-seed-smoke`.

Repro: run `mix test` a handful of times without a fixed seed; or bisect with the seeds that fail.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 6f961b88-5db6-4252-8e15-09ba44b83d36
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/6f961b88-5db6-4252-8e15-09ba44b83d36.jsonl

Commit: 3a725fb (branch mutagen-de1/coverage-tmp-flake, worktree mutagen_ex-coverage-tmp-flake)
Files touched: test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs
Tests added: "r7: no disk writes (broader surface, s7)" describe — new guard test
  "ambient mutagen_ex_* churn in global tmp does not reach the isolated surface (mutagen-de1)";
  existing r7 test reworked to snapshot a private per-test tmp dir.
Verification stubs flipped: none. mutagen-de1 has no Advances: field (pure bug fix).
  The coverage r7 contract is verified by spec stub v4 (mutagen.coverage.r7 ->
  "mix test --only spike test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs"), already execute: true
  and untouched.
Deferred must requirements: none for this ticket. Sibling flake in mutation_runner_test r11
  (same root cause, different file) filed as mutagen-0vv (out of scope here).

Summary: ROOT CAUSE — the stubbed CoverageRunner r7 disk-write test snapshotted the
shared global System.tmp_dir!() and, via mutagen_attributable_tmp/1's umbrella "mutagen_ex"
prefix, flagged ANY mutagen_ex_-prefixed entry appearing in the ~microsecond pre/post
window. CoverageRunner is fully stubbed in this test (CoverFakeOk/ExUnitFake) and writes
no tmp — verified: lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex has zero File.*/System.tmp_dir calls.
So the assertion had zero true-positive capacity but matched ambient mutagen_ex_* churn from
other lanes (C1 :spike mutagen_ex_c1_*, archive install mutagen_ex_archive_*, baseline/
beam_cache/memory unit tests) and concurrent processes on shared hardware — the
order/timing-dependent false positive that flaked make smoke under random seeds.

Confirmed empirically by instrumenting DiskSnapshot.diff across 30 suite runs: the disk-content
diffs (added/modified/removed over lib/_build/cover/host) were ALWAYS empty; the only
mutagen-attributable hit was tmp_added=["mutagen_ex_c1_131"] — a C1-lane entry, not the runner.

FIX — snapshot the tmp surface against a private, freshly-created per-test directory; sibling
lanes and other processes create their entries in global tmp at the same level, never inside
the isolated subdir, so ambient churn cannot reach it. The disk-content surfaces stay global
and real (they never churned). The REAL runner's no-global-tmp-write contract (r7) remains
verified by the C1 spike (v4) and the :cover_integration real-cover test, both of which drive
the genuine :cover lifecycle. No spec change. Added a deterministic regression guard that
reproduces the exact ambient-global-tmp churn and asserts the isolated surface is immune.

VERIFICATION: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; coverage_runner_test 17/17;
full suite (seed 0) 641 tests, 0 failures; 12 random-seed full-suite runs -> 0 coverage_runner
failures. mix spec.check: coverage subject passes. The 2 spec.check errors (mutagen.ast stale
grep target 'defp alias_to_module|find_module_body' — functions no longer exist; mutagen.cli
archive-install telemetry.app) are PRE-EXISTING on main and unrelated to this change (touched
no ast/cli files).

title: coverage_runner_test order-dependent tmp-leak flake under random seed
